name: run_shell
description: Execute a program with argv arguments. Does NOT invoke a shell interpreter, so shell metacharacters like `;`, `&&`, `|` are treated as literal arguments. In the default restricted mode, only whitelisted commands are allowed: python/python3, pip/pip3 (normalized to python -m pip), node/npm/npx, read-only git, and scoped filesystem commands like pwd/ls/cat/mkdir/cp/mv. If the user loosens shell access in Settings, arbitrary bare commands may be allowed, with optional per-call confirmation. Output is capped at 64 KB per stream. Default timeout 30s, max 300s. For Windows paths in JSON strings, escape backslashes (for example "E:\\Starting\\script.py") or use forward slashes.

program.description: Program to execute. In restricted mode it must be one of: python, python3, pip, pip3, node, npm, npx, git, pwd, ls, cat, mkdir, cp, mv. pip/pip3 are normalized to python/python3 -m pip. Simple aliases copy->cp and move->mv are also accepted. In less-restricted shell access modes, any bare command name may be allowed. No shell interpreter is invoked automatically.

args.description: Arguments passed directly as argv (no shell interpretation). Defaults to []. In restricted mode, git first arg must be a read-only subcommand (status, diff, log, show, ls-files, rev-parse, branch, tag, config, remote, describe, blame), and filesystem-command paths are resolved relative to cwd and must stay inside registered source roots.

cwd.description: Working directory for the command. In restricted mode it must resolve inside a registered source directory. In less-restricted shell access modes, any existing directory may be allowed.

timeout_secs.description: Timeout in seconds. Default 30, min 1, max 300. Values outside this range are clamped.
